title: Truncate String with Ellipsis
description: Truncates a string to a specified length and appends an ellipsis if it exceeds the limit.
explanation: The utility provides two tightly related functions for truncating strings: one by characters and another by lines. It handles edge cases like word boundaries, custom ellipsis, and line length, making it useful for UI text rendering where space is constrained. The modern API supports optional parameters for flexibility without external dependencies.

title: Unflatten Nested Arrays by Path
description: Reconstructs a nested array structure from flat entries paired with their path indices.
explanation: This utility uses a Map-free approach with nested arrays to reconstruct a tree from flat entries with path strings. It handles sparse arrays and avoids mutating the input by building the structure from scratch.

title: Viewport Intersection Observer Utility
description: Creates a reusable IntersectionObserver that tracks whether DOM elements enter or exit the viewport with minimal reflows.
explanation: This utility abstracts the IntersectionObserver API into a reusable component that tracks which elements are visible in the viewport. It minimizes reflows by only triggering callbacks when state changes occur, making it efficient for lazy-loading, animations, or viewport-based triggers.

title: Circular Buffer with Fixed Capacity
description: A fixed-capacity buffer that overwrites oldest entries when full, providing O(1) enqueue/dequeue via circular indexing.
explanation: Uses circular indexing to achieve O(1) enqueue and dequeue operations without shifting an array. The fixed capacity makes it ideal for streaming data where memory usage must be bounded. Circular buffers are widely used in audio processing, network routers, and logging systems.

title: Memoize Function Results
description: Memoizes function results to avoid recomputation for identical inputs.
explanation: Uses a Map for O(1) cache lookups and stores results by serialized arguments. The default stringification handles primitives and simple objects/arrays, while the custom key function allows for more nuanced caching strategies like deep equality checks. This avoids recomputation overhead for pure functions with expensive operations.

title: Clamp a Number Within a Range
description: Ensures a number stays within specified minimum and maximum bounds.
explanation: The clamp function uses Math.min and Math.max to bound a value, ensuring it never exceeds the specified range. It’s useful for UI sliders, progress bars, or any input that must stay within valid thresholds. The wrap function rotates values cyclically, ideal for circular carousels or wrapping time around a 24-hour clock.

title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
description: Executes an async function multiple times with exponentially increasing delays between retries on failure.
explanation: The utility wraps an async function and retries it on failure using exponential backoff. It starts with a short delay and doubles it each retry until reaching a cap, preventing server overload. This pattern is useful for transient errors like network glitches or rate limits.

title: Strong Password Validator
description: Validates if a password meets common security requirements.
explanation: The isStrongPassword function uses regex patterns to check for required character types, providing flexible validation through an options object. isStrongPasswordWithEntropy adds entropy calculation to measure password unpredictability, helping avoid weak but complex passwords.

title: Deep Merge Objects
description: Recursively merges two or more objects without mutating the originals.
explanation: Uses Reflect.ownKeys to capture all enumerable keys including symbols, then builds a new object with descriptors to preserve property attributes. Recursion handles nested objects, while Object.create(null) prevents prototype pollution. This ensures safe, non-mutating merges even with complex object graphs.

title: Human-Readable Relative Time Formatter
description: Converts a date into a human-readable relative time string like 'just now', '5 minutes ago', or 'in 2 hours'.
explanation: The function calculates the time difference in seconds between the target date and now, then uses thresholds for each unit (seconds, minutes, hours, etc.) to return the most appropriate human-readable string. It handles both past and future dates gracefully and avoids complex dependencies by using basic arithmetic and conditional logic.

title: Mask Email Address
description: Masks part of an email address for privacy while preserving the domain.
explanation: The maskEmail function preserves privacy by showing only the first few characters of an email while masking the rest with asterisks. It handles edge cases like missing @ signs and ensures the masked portion is readable. The truncate function complements this by safely shortening strings for display purposes without breaking words.

title: Split Array at Given Index
description: Splits an array into two parts at a specified index.
explanation: The `splitAtIndex` function leverages native `Array.slice` for efficient partitioning, while `splitAtDelimiter` manually iterates to handle arbitrary delimiters. Both are useful for data transformation tasks such as splitting logs, parsing streams, or reorganizing datasets without external libraries.
